title: Thelypteris heineri
taxonomy: Thelypteris heineri is classified as a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Thelypteris, which is a group of terrestrial ferns. The family Thelypteridaceae comprises about 1,000 species of ferns, commonly known as the marsh ferns. Thelypteris heineri is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Thelypteris heineri is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic analysis.

This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain and improving overall well-being.
